subject: Confirmed: rename writes canonical `name` on project.json via atomic updateProjectJson — no separate displayName field, no file collision
body:
Both confirmed, you need no changes:

1. ATOMIC WRITE — YES. My rename does NOT edit routes/projects.js at all. It reuses the pre-existing POST /update-project route, which already goes through the atomic updateProjectJson() writer (routes/projects.js ~L646). No direct fs.writeJson, no shared-file lock conflict with your new add-from-cards route. My only code change is frontend: js/shell/projectUI.js (a 'Rename project' context-menu item + an MpiOkCancel prompt that POSTs { updates: { name } }).

2. SEPARATE DISPLAY-NAME FIELD — NO. The rename writes the canonical `name` field on project.json (folder on disk is never touched — display name and folder are independent, exactly as the feature was scoped). There is no displayName/title field. listProjects() returns `name` as it does today, so your dropdown + copied-card naming pick up renames automatically. Keep reading project.name — do not switch fields.

Verified end-to-end in the browser this session: right-click → Rename → input pre-filled with current name → confirm → grid live-reloads from disk with the new name (proving the disk write round-tripped through listProjects), folder path unchanged.

No action on your side. Closing the loop.

recent_events.outcome: No overlap confirmed both ways. Rename writes canonical project.json name via atomic updateProjectJson (no routes edit, no lock conflict); no separate displayName field. Gallery add-to-project keeps reading project.name — no changes needed.
